---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# cgjrapp

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrapp/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrapp/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Codecov test coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrapp/graph/badge.svg)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrapp)
<!-- badges: end -->

`cgjrapp` is a Shiny dashboard that visualises governance data 
for the institutional chapter of the World Bank's
**Country Growth and Jobs Report (CGJR)**. It enables country-level
benchmarking across four governance clusters and thirteen subclusters,
drawing on indicator data from the companion
[`cgjrdata`](https://github.com/WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrdata)
package.

## Features

- **Overview tab** — evolution of CTF scores over time for a selected
  country, with peer, regional, and income-group benchmarks overlaid.
- **Benchmarking thresholds** — switch between four modes via a sidebar
  dropdown: relative quartiles, relative terciles, absolute quartiles,
  and absolute terciles.
- **Zone legend** — colour-coded weak / emerging / strong capacity zones
  always visible in the chart legend.
- **Interactive charts** — powered by `plotly` for hover tooltips and
  zoom.

## Prerequisites

`cgjrapp` depends on the private `cgjrdata` package. Install it first:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrdata")
```

## Installation

Install the development version of `cgjrapp` from GitHub:

``` r
pak::pak("WB-PIDA-Data-Science-Shop/cgjrapp")
```

## Usage

``` r
library(cgjrapp)

run_cgjrapp()
```

This launches the Shiny dashboard in your default browser. Use the
sidebar to select a **primary country**, optional **peer countries**,
**region**, **income group**, **year range**, and **benchmarking
threshold** mode.

## Data

All indicator data is sourced from `cgjrdata::institutional_averages_tbl`
and related exports. The four governance clusters are:

| Cluster | Subclusters |
|---|---|
| Institutional Environment | Degree of Integrity, Transparency & Accountability, Justice & Rule of Law, Social Cohesion |
| Political Institutions | … |
| Centre of Government | … |
| Sectors & Service Delivery | … |

## Development

``` r
# Load all functions without installing
devtools::load_all()

# Run tests
devtools::test()

# Full R CMD check
devtools::check()

# Rebuild README
devtools::build_readme()
```

## License

MIT © World Bank DICE Data Science Shop
